content:
# Exodus Chapter 37 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 108–109; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 80–81 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_037_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_037_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_037_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_037_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — EXECUTION AND REORDERING:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 37 across pages 108–109, continuing the construction narrative as Bezalel builds the sacred furniture: the ark, the table, the lampstand, and the incense altar. The chapter closely mirrors the instructions of chapters 25 and 30, shifting from "you shall make" to "and he made" (va-ya'as). Bezalel is named only in verse 1; thereafter the subject is an anonymous "he," subordinating the craftsman to the craft. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 80–81, headed CAP. XXVII–XXVIII) dramatically rearranges: MT Exodus 37 corresponds roughly to LXX 37:1–21 (=MT 38:1–7, the bronze altar and laver) and portions of LXX 38 (=MT 36 tabernacle construction). The MT's furniture-making account in chapter 37 is largely absent from Vaticanus at this point, having been folded into the instruction chapters (LXX Exodus 25–26). This is the most significant divergence between MT and LXX in the tabernacle narrative.

---

## The Ark of the Covenant (vv. 1–9)

**1** And Bezalel made [va-ya'as Betsal'el] the ark [et-ha-aron] of acacia wood [atsei shittim]; two cubits and a half [ammatayim va-khetsi] its length [orkho], and a cubit and a half [ve-ammah va-khetsi] its width [rokhbo], and a cubit and a half [ve-ammah va-khetsi] its height [qomato].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּעַשׂ בְּצַלְאֵל (va-ya'as Betsal'el) = "and Bezalel made" — the only verse in the chapter naming the craftsman; thereafter the anonymous "he" carries the action. אֲרוֹן (aron) = "ark/chest." **LXX:** Vaticanus lacks this verse at the corresponding location; the ark-making is absorbed into the instruction chapters.

**2** And he overlaid it [va-yetsappehu] with pure gold [zahav tahor] from inside and from outside [mi-bayit u-mi-khuts], and he made [va-ya'as] for it a rim of gold [zer zahav] all around [saviv].

> **Key terms:** וַיְצַפֵּהוּ (va-yetsappehu) = "and he overlaid it" — Piel of צפה; gold sheeting applied to the wood. זֵר (zer) = "rim/border/crown" — a decorative molding encircling the top. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**3** And he cast [va-yitsoq] for it four rings of gold [arba tabe'ot zahav] on its four feet [al arba pa'amotav], and two rings [u-shtei tabe'ot] on its one side [al-tsal'o ha-akhat] and two rings [u-shtei tabe'ot] on its second side [al-tsal'o ha-shenit].

> **Key terms:** פַּעֲמֹתָיו (pa'amotav) = "its feet/corners" — from פעם; the base corners where rings are mounted. טַבְּעֹת (tabe'ot) = "rings" — from טבע ("to sink/impress"); cast rings for the carrying poles. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**4** And he made [va-ya'as] poles [baddei] of acacia wood [atsei shittim], and he overlaid them [va-yetsap otam] with gold [zahav].

> **Key terms:** בַּדֵּי (baddei) = "poles/staves" — from בד ("separate/alone"); the carrying poles that remain in the rings. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**5** And he brought [va-yave] the poles [et-ha-baddim] into the rings [ba-tabe'ot] on the sides of the ark [al tsal'ot ha-aron], to carry [laset] the ark [et-ha-aron].

> **Key terms:** לָשֵׂאת (laset) = "to carry" — infinitive construct of נשׂא; the ark is always carried, never placed on a cart. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**6** And he made [va-ya'as] an atonement-cover [kapporet] of pure gold [zahav tahor]; two cubits and a half [ammatayim va-khetsi] its length [orkhah] and a cubit and a half [ve-ammah va-khetsi] its width [rokhbah].

> **Key terms:** כַּפֹּרֶת (kapporet) = "atonement-cover/mercy seat" — from כפר ("to cover/atone"); not merely a lid but the place where atonement occurs. **LXX:** ἱλαστήριον ("place of propitiation") in the instruction parallel (Exod 25:17 LXX).

**7** And he made [va-ya'as] two cherubim [shnei kheruvim] of gold [zahav] — of hammered-work [miqshah] he made them [asah otam] — from the two ends [mi-shnei qetsot] of the atonement-cover [ha-kapporet]:

> **Key terms:** מִקְשָׁה (miqshah) = "hammered-work" — from קשׁה; beaten from a single piece, not cast separately. כְּרוּבִים (kheruvim) = "cherubim" — guardian figures flanking the divine presence. **LXX:** ἐκ τοῦ ἱλαστηρίου ("from the mercy seat"), matching the single-piece construction.

**8** one cherub [keruv ekhad] from this end [mi-qatseh mi-zeh] and one cherub [u-kheruv ekhad] from that end [mi-qatseh mi-zeh]; from the atonement-cover [min-ha-kapporet] he made [asah] the cherubim [et-ha-kheruvim] from its two ends [mi-shnei qetsotav].

> **Key terms:** מִקָּצֶה מִזֶּה (mi-qatseh mi-zeh) = "from [the] end from this [side]" — emphatic spatial markers; each cherub rises from an opposite end. **LXX:** mirrors the instruction text in Exodus 25.

**9** And the cherubim were [va-yihyu ha-kheruvim] spreading out [porsei] wings [khenafayim] upward [le-ma'lah], covering [sokhakhim] with their wings [be-khanfeihem] over the atonement-cover [al-ha-kapporet], and their faces [u-fneihem] each to its brother [ish el-akhiv]; toward the atonement-cover [el-ha-kapporet] were the faces of the cherubim [penei ha-kheruvim].

> **Key terms:** סֹכְכִים (sokhakhim) = "covering/screening" — Qal participle of סכך; the wings create a canopy over the mercy seat. אִישׁ אֶל־אָחִיו (ish el-akhiv) = "each to his brother" — the cherubim face one another, framing the space of divine encounter. **LXX:** κατασκιάζοντα ("overshadowing") in the instruction parallel.

---

## The Table of the Presence (vv. 10–16)

**10** And he made [va-ya'as] the table [et-ha-shulkhan] of acacia wood [atsei shittim]; two cubits [ammatayim] its length [orkho] and a cubit [ve-ammah] its width [rokhbo] and a cubit and a half [ve-ammah va-khetsi] its height [qomato].

> **Key terms:** שֻׁלְחָן (shulkhan) = "table" — from שׁלח ("to send"); the table for the bread of the Presence. Dimensions match the instructions in 25:23. **LXX:** τράπεζαν in the instruction parallel.

**11** And he overlaid it [va-yetsap oto] with pure gold [zahav tahor], and he made [va-ya'as] for it a rim of gold [zer zahav] all around [saviv].

> **Key terms:** זֵר זָהָב (zer zahav) = "rim/crown of gold" — the same decorative molding as on the ark. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**12** And he made [va-ya'as] for it a border [misgeret] of a handbreadth [tofakh] all around [saviv], and he made [va-ya'as] a rim of gold [zer zahav] for its border [le-misgareto] all around [saviv].

> **Key terms:** מִסְגֶּרֶת (misgeret) = "border/frame" — from סגר ("to close/enclose"); a raised edge around the table, a handbreadth wide. טֹפַח (tofakh) = "handbreadth" — approximately 3 inches. **LXX:** στεφάνην ("wreath/crown") in the instruction parallel.

**13** And he cast [va-yitsoq] for it four rings of gold [arba tabe'ot zahav], and he put [va-yitten] the rings [et-ha-tabe'ot] on the four corners [al arba ha-pe'ot] which are on its four legs [asher le-arba raglav].

> **Key terms:** פֵּאֹת (pe'ot) = "corners" — from פאה; the four corners where the legs meet the tabletop. רַגְלָיו (raglav) = "its legs/feet." **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**14** Opposite the border [le-ummat ha-misgeret] were the rings [ha-tabe'ot] — holders [batim] for the poles [la-baddim] to carry [laset] the table [et-ha-shulkhan].

> **Key terms:** בָּתִּים (batim) = "holders/housings" — literally "houses"; the rings serve as housings for the carrying poles. לְעֻמַּת (le-ummat) = "opposite/close to" — the rings are positioned near the border. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**15** And he made [va-ya'as] the poles [et-ha-baddim] of acacia wood [atsei shittim], and he overlaid them [va-yetsap otam] with gold [zahav], to carry [laset] the table [et-ha-shulkhan].

> **Key terms:** Same construction pattern as the ark's poles — acacia wood overlaid with gold. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**16** And he made [va-ya'as] the vessels [et-ha-kelim] which are on the table [asher al-ha-shulkhan] — its dishes [et-qe'arotav] and its ladles [ve-et-kappotav] and its bowls [ve-et-menaqiyyotav] and the jugs [ve-et-ha-qesavot] by which it is poured out [asher yussak bahen] — pure gold [zahav tahor].

> **Key terms:** קְעָרֹת (qe'arot) = "dishes/plates" — for the bread of the Presence. כַּפֹּת (kappot) = "ladles/spoons" — literally "palms"; for incense. מְנַקִּיּוֹת (menaqiyyot) = "bowls" — possibly for drink offerings. קְשָׂוֹת (qesavot) = "jugs/pitchers" — for pouring libations; אֲשֶׁר יֻסַּךְ בָּהֵן (asher yussak bahen) = "by which it is poured" — Hophal of נסך. **LXX:** τρυβλία, θυΐσκας, σπονδεῖα in the instruction parallel.

---

## The Lampstand of Pure Gold (vv. 17–24)

**17** And he made [va-ya'as] the lampstand [et-ha-menorah] of pure gold [zahav tahor]; of hammered-work [miqshah] he made [asah] the lampstand [et-ha-menorah] — its base [yerekah] and its shaft [ve-qanah], its cups [gevi'eha], its knobs [kaphtor'eha], and its blossoms [u-frakheiha] were from it [mimmenah hayu].

> **Key terms:** מְנוֹרָה (menorah) = "lampstand" — from נור/נר ("light/lamp"). מִקְשָׁה (miqshah) = "hammered-work" — the entire lampstand beaten from a single talent of gold. יְרֵכָהּ (yerekah) = "its thigh/base" — the heavy foot. גְּבִיעֶיהָ (gevi'eha) = "its cups" — almond-blossom-shaped cups. **LXX:** λυχνίαν in the instruction parallel; Vaticanus omits the execution account.

**18** And six branches [ve-shishah qanim] going out [yotse'im] from its sides [mi-tsiddeha] — three branches of the lampstand [sheloshah qenei menorah] from its one side [mi-tsiddah ha-ekhad] and three branches of the lampstand [u-shloshah qenei menorah] from its second side [mi-tsiddah ha-sheni].

> **Key terms:** קָנִים (qanim) = "branches/reeds" — from קנה; the six lateral branches, three per side. **LXX:** the instruction parallel in Exod 25:32 LXX matches.

**19** Three cups [sheloshah gevi'im] shaped-like-almonds [meshuqqadim] on the one branch [ba-qaneh ha-ekhad] — a knob [kaphtor] and a blossom [va-ferakh] — and three cups [u-shloshah gevi'im] shaped-like-almonds [meshuqqadim] on the other branch [ba-qaneh ha-ekhad] — a knob [kaphtor] and a blossom [va-ferakh]; thus [ken] for the six branches [le-sheshet ha-qanim] going out [ha-yotse'im] from the lampstand [min-ha-menorah].

> **Key terms:** מְשֻׁקָּדִים (meshuqqadim) = "shaped-like-almonds" — Pual participle of שׁקד; each cup imitates an almond blossom. כַּפְתֹּר וָפֶרַח (kaphtor va-ferakh) = "knob and blossom" — ornamental bulge and flower on each branch. **LXX:** ἐκτετυπωμένοι καρυΐσκους ("embossed with nuts") in the instruction parallel.

**20** And on the lampstand [u-va-menorah] — four cups [arba'ah gevi'im] shaped-like-almonds [meshuqqadim], its knobs [kaphtor'eha] and its blossoms [u-frakheiha].

> **Key terms:** The central shaft has four cups, one more than each branch — the trunk exceeds the limbs. **LXX:** matches the instruction parallel.

**21** And a knob [ve-kaphtor] under the two branches [takhat shnei ha-qanim] from it [mimmennu], and a knob [ve-kaphtor] under the two branches [takhat shnei ha-qanim] from it [mimmennu], and a knob [ve-kaphtor] under the two branches [takhat shnei ha-qanim] from it [mimmennu] — for the six branches [le-sheshet ha-qanim] going out [ha-yotse'im] from it [mimmenah].

> **Key terms:** Three knobs at the three junction points where each pair of branches meets the central shaft — structural and decorative nodes. מִמֶּנּוּ (mimmennu) = "from it" — repeated three times, insisting that every branch grows organically from the shaft. **LXX:** matches the instruction parallel.

**22** Their knobs [kaphtoreihem] and their branches [u-qenotam] were from it [mimmenah hayu]; all of it [kullahh] one hammered-work [miqshah akhat] of pure gold [zahav tahor].

> **Key terms:** כֻּלָּהּ מִקְשָׁה אַחַת (kullahh miqshah akhat) = "all of it one hammered-work" — the theological emphasis: a single piece, total unity. **LXX:** ὅλη τορευτὴ μία ("entirely one turned piece") in the instruction parallel.

**23** And he made [va-ya'as] its lamps [et-neroteiha] — seven [shiv'ah] — and its tongs [u-malqakheiha] and its fire-pans [u-makhtoteiha] of pure gold [zahav tahor].

> **Key terms:** נֵרֹתֶיהָ (neroteiha) = "its lamps" — seven individual oil lamps set on the branches and shaft. מַלְקָחֶיהָ (malqakheiha) = "its tongs/snuffers" — from לקח ("to take"); for trimming wicks. מַחְתֹּתֶיהָ (makhtoteiha) = "its fire-pans/censers" — from חתה ("to snatch fire"); for removing ash. **LXX:** matches the instruction parallel.

**24** Of a talent [kikkar] of pure gold [zahav tahor] he made [asah] it [otah] and all [ve-et kol] its vessels [keleha].

> **Key terms:** כִּכָּר (kikkar) = "talent" — approximately 75 pounds / 34 kg of pure gold for the entire lampstand and its accessories. **LXX:** τάλαντον χρυσίου καθαροῦ ("a talent of pure gold") in the instruction parallel.

---

## The Altar of Incense (vv. 25–28)

**25** And he made [va-ya'as] the altar of incense [et-mizbach ha-qetoret] of acacia wood [atsei shittim]; a cubit [ammah] its length [orkho] and a cubit [ve-ammah] its width [rokhbo] — square [ravua'] — and two cubits [ve-ammatayim] its height [qomato]; from it [mimmennu] were its horns [qarnottav].

> **Key terms:** מִזְבַּח הַקְּטֹרֶת (mizbach ha-qetoret) = "altar of the incense" — from קטר ("to burn incense"); the golden altar that stands before the veil. רָבוּעַ (ravua') = "square" — a perfect square footprint. קַרְנֹתָיו (qarnottav) = "its horns" — projections at each corner, integral to the altar (מִמֶּנּוּ, "from it"). **LXX:** θυσιαστήριον θυμιάματος in the instruction parallel (Exod 30:1 LXX); Vaticanus omits the execution account here.

**26** And he overlaid it [va-yetsap oto] with pure gold [zahav tahor] — its top [et-gaggo] and its walls [ve-et-qirotav] all around [saviv] and its horns [ve-et-qarnottav]; and he made [va-ya'as] for it a rim of gold [zer zahav] all around [saviv].

> **Key terms:** גַּגּוֹ (gaggo) = "its top/roof" — from גג; the upper surface where incense is burned. קִירֹתָיו (qirotav) = "its walls" — all four sides covered in gold. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**27** And two rings of gold [u-shtei tabe'ot zahav] he made [asah] for it beneath [mi-takhat] its rim [le-zeiro], on its two sides [al shnei tsiddav] — on its two flanks [al shnei tsiddav] — for holders [le-vatim] for poles [le-vaddim] to carry it [laset oto] with them [bahem].

> **Key terms:** לְזֵרוֹ (le-zeiro) = "to its rim" — the rings are placed just below the decorative molding. The specification of "two sides" (not four) means two rings, one per side, for a single pair of carrying poles. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

**28** And he made [va-ya'as] the poles [et-ha-baddim] of acacia wood [atsei shittim], and he overlaid them [va-yetsap otam] with gold [zahav].

> **Key terms:** Same construction as all sacred poles — acacia wood with gold overlay. **LXX:** absent at this location in Vaticanus.

---

## The Sacred Oil and Incense (v. 29)

**29** And he made [va-ya'as] the oil of the holy anointing [et-shemen ha-mishkhah qodesh] and the incense of the spices [ve-et-qetoret ha-sammim], pure [tahor] — the work of a perfumer [ma'aseh roqeakh].

> **Key terms:** שֶׁמֶן הַמִּשְׁחָה קֹדֶשׁ (shemen ha-mishkhah qodesh) = "the oil of the anointing of holiness" — the sacred anointing oil whose recipe was given in 30:22–33. קְטֹרֶת הַסַּמִּים (qetoret ha-sammim) = "incense of the spices" — the holy incense blend of 30:34–38. מַעֲשֵׂה רֹקֵחַ (ma'aseh roqeakh) = "work of a perfumer/apothecary" — from רקח ("to compound spices"); a distinct craft from metalwork or weaving. **LXX:** Vaticanus omits this verse at the corresponding location; the anointing oil and incense formulas appear only in the instruction text (LXX Exod 30).

---

## Manuscript Notes

**Leningrad Codex (pp. 108–109):** The text is written in the standard three-column format. The chapter continues directly from the tabernacle curtains and boards of chapter 36, with no major paragraph break (setumah/petuhah) before verse 1 in some traditions. The parashah marker is visible. The text is fully pointed with Tiberian vocalization and accents.

**Codex Vaticanus (pp. 80–81):** These pages are headed CAP. XXVII and CAP. XXVIII, confirming the well-known divergence. The LXX at this point contains the tabernacle court construction (corresponding to MT 38:9–20) and the priestly garments (corresponding to MT 28), not the furniture of MT 37. The entire furniture-execution narrative of MT 37 is effectively absent from LXX Exodus, having been subsumed into the instruction chapters. This represents one of the most substantial structural differences between MT and LXX in the entire Pentateuch — the LXX does not repeat the furniture construction, treating the instruction as sufficient.
